title: Create a Basic HAProxy Ingress
description:
## Create a Basic HAProxy Ingress

HAProxy Ingress uses standard Kubernetes `Ingress` resources. The controller picks them up when `spec.ingressClassName` is set to `haproxy`.

**Your task:**

A Deployment and Service named `web-app` are running in the `haproxy-lab` namespace on port `80`. Create an `Ingress` resource named `web-app-ingress` in the same namespace that:
- Uses `ingressClassName: haproxy`
- Routes all requests with path prefix `/app` to the `web-app` Service on port `80`
- Uses `pathType: Prefix`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get ingress web-app-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.ingressClassName}'
kubectl get ingress web-app-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.rules[0].http.paths[0].path}'
```

hints.title: Standard Ingress with ingressClassName
hints.body: Use spec.ingressClassName to bind to the HAProxy controller. The path and backend are defined under spec.rules.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: web-app-ingress
  namespace: haproxy-lab
spec:
  ingressClassName: haproxy
  rules:
  - http:
      paths:
      - path: /app
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: web-app
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

setup_commands.command: kubectl create deployment web-app --image=nginx:alpine -n haproxy-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment web-app --port=80 -n haproxy-lab 2>/dev/null || true

validation.description: Check that the Ingress exists, uses the haproxy class, and routes /app to web-app

commands.command: kubectl get ingress web-app-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get ingress web-app-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.ingressClassName}'
